title: cytisus scoparius
intro: Cytisus scoparius, also known as broom or Scotch broom, is a perennial legume native to Europe and western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the nervous system, improve circulation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of broom include its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Broom is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in herbal capsules or topically applied as a cream or oil.
